entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The grain crop studied for charcoal rot development

entities.name: CSH 6
entities.description: A specific hybrid of sorghum used in the experiments

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing charcoal rot

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE STRESS
entities.description: Environmental stress caused by lack of soil moisture

entities.name: CHARCOAL ROT
entities.description: A disease in sorghum characterized by stalk rot and lodging

entities.name: PERCENTAGE LODGING
entities.description: The metric used to quantify the development of charcoal rot

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, which is reduced by charcoal rot

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The research approach used to study the effects of the pathogen and moisture stress

entities.name: FURROW IRRIGATION
entities.description: A technique used to eliminate soil moisture stress

entities.name: SOIL FUMIGATION
entities.description: A technique used to eliminate the pathogen from the soil

entities.name: METHYL BROMIDE
entities.description: The chemical agent used for soil fumigation

relationships.description: interacts to increase charcoal rot development
